summary: Gruber examines Apple's announcement of three third-party Find My network products, noting that none are immediately available — Belkin's earbuds and Chipolo's tracker won't ship until June, and VanMoof's bikes have months-long delivery backlogs. He argues this premature announcement only makes sense if Apple is about to unveil AirTags at an imminent event, wanting to establish the 'Find My supports third parties' narrative first. He notes that Apple also has iOS 14.5 and other OS updates ready to ship, including the high-profile app tracking transparency feature. However, as of his writing, Apple had not announced any upcoming event, breaking their established pattern of 7-8 day advance notice for online events, leaving him suspecting something may have gone awry with their timeline.
key_points: None of the three announced Find My third-party products are immediately available, undermining the announcement's impact
The premature announcement suggests Apple wants to frame Find My as an open ecosystem before unveiling AirTags
iOS 14.5's app tracking transparency feature likely needs an event-style rollout where Apple can explain it on their own terms
Apple's established pattern of announcing online events 7-8 days in advance has been broken, suggesting a scheduling disruption
The combination of ready-to-ship OS updates and premature third-party announcements points to a delayed product event
money_quotes: It only makes sense to me if Apple is on the cusp of announcing AirTags very soon, and wanted to get their 'Find My supports third-party products too' story out the door beforehand, even if the products aren't shipping for months.
Putting Find My integration in VanMoof's bikes is cool, but they're not exactly mainstream products.
iOS 14.5 is the version that introduces the much-publicized 'opt-in to allow apps to track you' changes — a feature I suspect Apple wants to explain on their own terms during an event.
Then, at the end of the show, Tim Cook can conclude by telling everyone these OS updates are all available now.
But it's starting to feel like something has gone awry.
key_insight: Apple's premature announcement of unavailable third-party Find My products strongly suggests an imminent AirTags event, but the absence of any event announcement hints at a disrupted timeline.
